content: You need a screenshot utility. A super FREE one is at https://www.reasyze.com/, called sniptool. A fancier one I like, that is also free, but requires a modest learning curve, is at https://portableapps.com/apps/graphics_pictures/fscportable
creator: davidsk

content:
Thunderbird should be using the authentication method oauth, not password of any sort for gmail.

This process has some requirements that those that change default security settings tend to crash into. See https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/automatic-conversion-google-mail-accounts-oauth20
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Per the instruction in link: https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/automatic-conversion-google-mail-accounts-oauth20

  • Ensure that your Thunderbird Gmail account is set to OAuth2 – YES, for both incoming and outgoing servers.
  • Cookies and JavaScript: Both are enabled.
  • Check if the proper authentication tokens have been saved: None are saved.
  • imap://imap.gmail.com (imap://imap.gmail.com): Not saved
  • smtp://smtp.gmail.com (smtp://smtp.gmail.com): Not Saved

it still fails with an error message: "Unable to login at the server. Probably wrong configuration, username or password."
creator: Hardip.Pannu
